tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting impaired renal function, likely acute or chronic kidney disease with possible nephrotic features. Blood glucose is normal but HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or altered red cell turnover. Urinalysis findings (brown, cloudy urine) may indicate underlying renal pathology. Prompt nephrology evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provider Organization: Azienda Sanitaria Locale n° 7 Musei
